steps.title: Identify what the sentence needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "It is widely known that MoMA ______ a vast collection of oil paintings." The blank is the main verb of the noun clause after that. A noun clause requires a finite verb — one that is fully inflected for tense and can stand as the main verb of a clause.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test all four choices against the finite-verb requirement:
  • Choice A — houses: finite simple-present verb that correctly serves as the main verb of the noun clause. ✓
  • Choice B — to have housed: violates the finite-verb rule — an infinitive phrase cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — housing: violates the finite-verb rule — a present participle cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — to house: violates the finite-verb rule — a bare infinitive cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
That matches Choice A — houses.
